title: psoralea hirta
intro: Psoralea hirta, also known as Indian breadroot or hoary psoralea, is a perennial plant native to the western parts of North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and support the immune system. The plant contains compounds like psoralen, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including psoriasis and vitiligo, and to enhance the effects of ultraviolet light therapy. Herbal preparations of Psoralea hirta can be made into capsules, tinctures, or teas for internal use, and the plant is also used topically in salves and creams to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
